spec_match.rationale: No-developer-visible-change feature — by definition the API doesn't expose the new behaviour, so the demo can only probe indirectly. Honest about the limit but doesn't fully match the spec's framing.

interactive.rationale: 2 buttons / 2 inputs / 0 selects / 0 ranges driving live JS — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates the feature-index blurb's key promise (overlap on change, incognito, memory, sqlite).

edge_cases.rationale: Some fallback / error handling is present but corner cases like removed-element behaviour, denied permission flow, or no-support browsers aren't all covered.

references.rationale: Page includes the chromestatus.com/feature/5126896685809664 link in the see-also section.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from the shared CSS variables and the layout follows the stage + controls editorial pattern.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: the user-visible behaviour is intentionally identical, so th
openQuestions.detail: The indexeddb-sqlite-backend-in-memory-contexts feature's headline framing — IndexedDB backed by SQLite for in-memory (Incognito) contexts — no web-visible API change, reliability improvement — calls out "the user-visible behaviour is intentionally identical, so the demo should probe reliability / corruption recovery". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: incognito vs regular comparison
openQuestions.detail: The indexeddb-sqlite-backend-in-memory-contexts feature's headline framing — IndexedDB backed by SQLite for in-memory (Incognito) contexts — no web-visible API change, reliability improvement — calls out "incognito vs regular comparison". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one realistic failure mode
openQuestions.detail: Add feature detection at the top of the script and surface the result. Handle the permission-denied / API-rejection case in the catch branch with a visible message. If the feature is flag-gated or origin-trial, show explicitly what the page does when the API is absent.

summary: incognito-idb under indexeddb-sqlite-backend-in-memory-contexts: 4 pass / 2 partial / 0 fail. Strong interactive surface, design system clean, spec story is partial — see open questions for the angles still missing.
